applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with use_block_ptr=False
applied_optimizations.description: Used torch.compile(mode='default') on entire forward method with torch._inductor.config.triton.use_block_ptr=False. This disables block pointer codegen that causes async_copy errors on ROCm. Achieved 1.07ms vs 1.21ms baseline - 1.13x speedup, SCORE: 61.3!

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton fused kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton kernel overhead too high for this workload

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile mode='max-autotune-no-cudagraphs'
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton compilation error with async operations

notes: Block pointers in Triton cause async_copy_global_to_local errors on ROCm gfx950
Disabling block pointers allows torch.compile to generate working Triton kernels
Fusing conv bias add with min+softmax provides the performance win
